title: smoke-cold rich irish pork dumplings
description: velvety irish dish featuring wonton skin, smoked pork, and a hint of mustard. this dish is ayurvedic sattvic-friendly.
instructions: in a large bowl, combine pork shoulder, onion, smoked paprika, apple cider vinegar, honey, salt, and black pepper. mix well.
let the mixture sit at room temperature for at least 30 minutes.
in a separate bowl, combine flour and grated carrots. mix well.
add the flour mixture to the pork mixture and mix until just combined. be careful not to overmix.
cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
to assemble the dumplings, lay a wonton skin on a clean surface. place a tablespoon of the pork mixture in the center of the skin.
dip your finger in a small amount of water and run it along the edges of the skin. fold the skin over the filling to form a triangle and press the edges together to seal the dumpling.
repeat with the remaining wonton skins and pork mixture.
in a large skillet or wok, heat 2 tablespoons of oil over medium-high heat.
when the oil is hot, add a few dumplings to the pan (do not overcrowd). cook for 2-3 minutes on each side, until the dumplings are golden brown.
to serve, place the dumplings on a plate and garnish with chopped parsley and a drizzle of honey.
Cook pork to an internal temperature of 63°C / 145°F and let it rest for 3 minutes before serving.
